perturbation.original_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached, and any amendments thereto may be filed without the necessity of filing additional joint filing agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Filing Agreement is attached, and any amendments thereto may be filed without the necessity of filing additional filing agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Joint Filing Agreement' is changed to 'Filing Agreement'. This introduces uncertainty because later in the document, the original term 'Joint Filing Agreement' is used. This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether all references to 'Joint Filing Agreement' also apply to the 'Filing Agreement'. This creates uncertainty in enforcement, as it is unclear whether the agreement to jointly file extends to agreements simply labelled 'Filing Agreement.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Filing Agreement is attached, and any amendments thereto may be filed without the necessity of filing additional filing agreements. This Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which shall constitute one and the same instrument. The execution and filing of this agreement shall not be construed as an admission that the below-named parties are a group or have acted as a group.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to a 'Joint Filing Agreement.' The changed text replaces this with 'Filing Agreement.' The contradicted text uses 'Filing Agreement' to refer to the same document as described in the changed text. However, the explanation states that later parts of the document use the original term 'Joint Filing Agreement.' This creates a contradiction because it becomes unclear whether all instances of 'Joint Filing Agreement' are also applicable to the document now referred to as 'Filing Agreement,' leading to ambiguity and potential disputes regarding the scope and applicability of the agreement. The misaligned terminology creates a direct conflict.

perturbation.explanation: Changing 'HPS INVESTMENT PARTNERS, LLC' to 'HPS HOLDING PARTNERS, LLC' introduces a contradiction because several entities in the agreement are defined by their relationship to the original 'HPS INVESTMENT PARTNERS, LLC'. This is a critical name change that creates ambiguity as the original entity name appears again and again, thereby undermining the contract's clarity and raising challenges regarding liability, obligation and agreement.
perturbation.location: HPS INVESTMENT PARTNERS, LLC signature block
perturbation.contradicted_location: MEZZANINE PARTNERS - OFFSHORE INVESTMENT MASTER FUND II, L.P. signature block
perturbation.contradicted_text: MEZZANINE PARTNERS - OFFSHORE INVESTMENT MASTER FUND II, L.P. By: HPS Mezzanine Partners II, LLC, its investment manager By: HPS Investment Partners, LLC, its sole member /s/ John Madden Name: John Madden Title: Authorized Signatory
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'HPS INVESTMENT PARTNERS, LLC' with 'HPS HOLDING PARTNERS, LLC'. The contradicted text uses 'HPS Investment Partners, LLC' as the sole member of 'HPS Mezzanine Partners II, LLC', the investment manager. Since the entity names are now different, the contradicted text no longer accurately reflects the changed entity. This misalignment creates a direct contradiction regarding the identity and role of the entity, undermining the agreement's clarity about who is authorized to act, therefore it is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Authorized Signatory' to 'Designated Signatory' when the signatory is signing on behalf of other parties introduces an in-text contradiction because the term 'Authorized Signatory' is used later in other signature blocks. It now becomes ambiguous which signatory roles are permitted by the agreement: only the ones named 'Designated' or the ones named 'Authorized' as well. Legal ambiguity arises regarding whether the signatures are legally binding, and whether those individuals hold legal authority.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HPS MEZZANINE PARTNERS II, LLC By: HPS Investment Partners, LLC, its sole member /s/ John Madden Name: John Madden Title: Authorized Signatory [Signature Page to Joint Filing Agreement]
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Authorized Signatory' to 'Designated Signatory' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text uses the term 'Authorized Signatory'. This misalignment of terminology introduces ambiguity and a clear conflict, making it uncertain whether the signatures are legally binding and which signatory roles are permitted. The definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' applies directly here, as the contract 'uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations'. The contradicted text uses 'Authorized Signatory', which is different from the changed text's 'Designated Signatory', creating a direct conflict and potential legal issues.
